item.title: 9 January 2027 LEGO Sets Detailed in New Leak
item.description:
Information on nine unannounced LEGO sets reportedly targeting a January 2027 launch has surfaced online. All of the newly leaked products are believed to be part of LEGO's Star Wars theme.

Dating back to the 1990s, the Star Wars line is by far the oldest licensed theme the Danish toymaker still updates today. 47 new LEGO Star Wars sets were launched or revealed in the first half of 2026 alone. More sets are rumored to follow in the second half, while reports of an early 2027 refresh have also circulated in the industry for some time.

Early 2027 LEGO Star Wars Line Reportedly Includes New X-Wing and Y-Wing Sets

On June 9, LEGO leaker "got.cooped" listed nine sets purported to belong to the early 2027 Star Wars line. So far, only three have been identified beyond their set numbers, with the wave reportedly including new replicas of Slave I, an X-wing, and a Y-wing. Slave I is best known in Star Wars lore as Boba Fett's gunship, while the X-wing remains one of the most iconic spacecraft designs in the franchise, commonly associated with the Rebels. The Y-wing is a more niche pick, known for its utilitarian design. The ship has appeared in two notably different forms across the franchise: a classic variant with a more complete outer hull, and the stripped-down Y-wing version commonly seen at Rebel outposts. In-universe, the latter design is typically explained as a practical modification, with crews removing the outer plating to make the craft easier to service and maintain.

All Newly Leaked LEGO Star Wars Sets

Theme

No

Set

Price

Pieces

Age

Release

Star Wars

30749

Slave I

$4.99

❓

❓

January 2027

Star Wars

75463

X-wing Microfighter

$14.99

❓

❓

January 2027

Star Wars

75464

❓

$29.99

❓

❓

January 2027

Star Wars

75466

❓

$79.99

❓

❓

January 2027

Star Wars

75470

❓

$79.99

❓

❓

January 2027

Star Wars

75476

❓

$29.99

❓

❓

January 2027

Star Wars

75478

Y-wing

$79.99

❓

❓

January 2027

Star Wars

75480

❓

$79.99

❓

❓

January 2027

Star Wars

75482

❓

$149.99

❓

❓

January 2027

The X-wing already enjoys extensive LEGO representation, reflecting its status as one of the most recognizable spacecraft in the Lucasfilm franchise. In 2026 alone, the Danish toymaker released two new models based on the famous ship: 75423 Luke's Red Five X-Wing Interactive Building Toy and 75460 New Republic X-Wing Starfighter. The former is a Smart Play set, and some of the newly leaked products may also belong to that line. Smart Play debuted in early 2026 and is not officially available worldwide yet, but it is expected to expand in the first half of 2027, with more products and broader market availability.

The newly leaked sets are all said to be targeting a January 2027 release. Based on LEGO's early 2026 offerings, January 1 is the most likely release date for all nine of them. The sets will reportedly be priced at between $4.99 and $149.99. The former sticker is reportedly attached to the latest Slave I model, implying the upcoming set will be a polybag.

Find the odd one out before the timer hits zero.

Round 1

Which one doesn't belong?

0 / 10
10.0 s

Results

0/5

Best: 0/5

—

More Games
Find the odd one out before the timer hits zero.
Easy (15s)Medium (10s)Hard (5s)Permadeath (5s)
Round 1 Which one doesn't belong?
0 / 10
10.0 s
Results
0/5
Best: 0/5 —
More Games

Beyond Star Wars , LEGO's leaked 2027 lineup so far includes new additions to the Shrek and Super Mario themes, among others. The previously announced K-Pop Demon Hunters and Hello Kitty themes have yet to receive any sets, though both are expected to either debut or be expanded in 2027.
item.content:encoded:
Information on nine unannounced LEGO sets reportedly targeting a January 2027 launch has surfaced online. All of the newly leaked products are believed to be part of LEGO's Star Wars theme.

Dating back to the 1990s, the Star Wars line is by far the oldest licensed theme the Danish toymaker still updates today. 47 new LEGO Star Wars sets were launched or revealed in the first half of 2026 alone. More sets are rumored to follow in the second half, while reports of an early 2027 refresh have also circulated in the industry for some time.

Early 2027 LEGO Star Wars Line Reportedly Includes New X-Wing and Y-Wing Sets

On June 9, LEGO leaker "got.cooped" listed nine sets purported to belong to the early 2027 Star Wars line. So far, only three have been identified beyond their set numbers, with the wave reportedly including new replicas of Slave I, an X-wing, and a Y-wing. Slave I is best known in Star Wars lore as Boba Fett's gunship, while the X-wing remains one of the most iconic spacecraft designs in the franchise, commonly associated with the Rebels. The Y-wing is a more niche pick, known for its utilitarian design. The ship has appeared in two notably different forms across the franchise: a classic variant with a more complete outer hull, and the stripped-down Y-wing version commonly seen at Rebel outposts. In-universe, the latter design is typically explained as a practical modification, with crews removing the outer plating to make the craft easier to service and maintain.

All Newly Leaked LEGO Star Wars Sets

Theme

No

Set

Price

Pieces

Age

Release

$4.99

January 2027

Star Wars

75482

$149.99

❓

❓

January 2027

The X-wing already enjoys extensive LEGO representation, reflecting its status as one of the most recognizable spacecraft in the Lucasfilm franchise. In 2026 alone, the Danish toymaker released two new models based on the famous ship: 75423 Luke's Red Five X-Wing Interactive Building Toy and 75460 New Republic X-Wing Starfighter. The former is a Smart Play set, and some of the newly leaked products may also belong to that line. Smart Play debuted in early 2026 and is not officially available worldwide yet, but it is expected to expand in the first half of 2027, with more products and broader market availability.

The newly leaked sets are all said to be targeting a January 2027 release. Based on LEGO's early 2026 offerings, January 1 is the most likely release date for all nine of them. The sets will reportedly be priced at between $4.99 and $149.99. The former sticker is reportedly attached to the latest Slave I model, implying the upcoming set will be a polybag.

Find the odd one out before the timer hits zero.

Round 1

Best: 0/5 —
More Games

Beyond Star Wars , LEGO's leaked 2027 lineup so far includes new additions to the Shrek and Super Mario themes, among others. The previously announced K-Pop Demon Hunters and Hello Kitty themes have yet to receive any sets, though both are expected to either debut or be expanded in 2027.

item.title: Destiny 2: Oblation Bloodline (Exotic Mission Walkthrough)
item.description:
Bloodline: Oblation is a secret Exotic Mission in Destiny 2 that takes you through a Hive fortress that's been infiltrated by Dredgeb Bael and the remaining forces of his Barant Imperium.

The hunt for Dredgen Call's long-lost gun begins, and with winding caverns and confusing mechanics, you might need a guide to help you reach the finish line to get Cull's Shadow (an Exotic Kinetic Fusion Rifle) for yourself.

Haven't got Oblation Bloodline unlocked? Click the link provided below for a guide on unlocking the mission, as this guide covers how to complete the mission itself.

How to Sabotage the Syndicate Drilling Operation in Destiny 2

After a rendezvous with Aunor, you will need to head down to the Hive structures to sabotage the Syndicate Drilling Operation. To begin, look to the left of the giant laser, and you'll find a room containing a Totality Enforcer. With him dead, you can engage the Manual Safety Override, which reveals three blue Cooling Tower Control Fuses you need to shoot .

How to Destroy the Cooling Pillars in Destiny 2

Cooling Pillar #1

You now need to return to the other platform, as this has the first Cooling Pillar that you need to destroy. The central device has four vents that open randomly, and when they're open, you can shoot them to destroy them. Once all four have been shot, use the launch pad to reach the next area.

Cooling Pillar #2 & #3

Once you've landed on the new platform, you'll want to eliminate the Totality Enforcer and immediately turn around to find another terminal you can interact with. This will open three more Cooling Tower Control Fuses on the left, right, and middle towers to destroy.

With these fuses destroyed, you can now shoot the 4 vents on the Cooling Pillar to the left and right. With them destroyed, it's time to move onto the path on the left, which has a room filled with Legion to kill. With them dead, interact with the terminal that says "WARNING: OVERLOAD", and take the launch pad to the next area.

How to Brave the Tunnels in Destiny 2

Once you cross over to the other side of the Hive fortress, you'll be attacked by heaps of Thrall and Acolytes. Once they're dead, you're going to find many tunnels, but they will all lead to dead ends. Instead, you need to navigate all the way to the top . At the very top, you can enter the tunnels through to the Soulfire Crux.

When you see Dredgen Bael's shadow, you can safely ignore it and instead look to your left to see Hive lanterns that you can jump on. Your goal is to platform all the way across to the other side and up the platforms to reach a red room, where you can drop down to a ledge on the other side to enter the room.

Follow the hallway down to find Bael's shadow again, but the room you enter will be locked. Turn around and look down the hallway, and you can find a crack in the stairs that you can drop down to find the next area. You can now continue on the path until you find the Soulfire Barrier.

How to Extinguish the Soulfire Barrier in Destiny 2

Once you kill the Hive enemies, there will be a Wizard called the Pyre Matron. You must kill the Pyre Matron to release the shields on the Soulfire Vessel . You can now kill the Soulfire Vessel with a melee or finisher to get the Soulfire Charge . Take the Soulfire Charge to the unlit pyre to ignite it, and repeat this in the left and right rooms.

The left room is accessed via the rock platforms instead of through a door.

How to Beat Valus Tau'rig in Destiny 2

You'll now be at the boss encounter against Valus Tau'rig, who is using Cull's Shadow. Valus has a first phase where you just need to fight him as normal. However, before you can kill him, Hive Wizards corrupt him, and thus begins his next phase.

To beat Valus Tau'rig, you need to kill ads until you see the text "He seeks a source of soulfire". Now, in either the left or right room, there will be Pyre Matrons and a Soulfire Vessel. You just need to kill them and light one of the three pyres with the Soulfire Charge to lower his shields .

Subscribe to the newsletter for more Destiny 2 guides

Get more from the game by subscribing to the newsletter for focused Destiny 2 coverage: precise mission walkthroughs, step-by-step tactics for encounters, and follow-up guides on Exotics and catalysts — everything tied to in-game unlocks like Cull's Shadow.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

For the second-half of his health bar, you will need to use a Soulfire Charge on two pyres, so follow the Hive Wizards into the ritual room and kill them so you can then kill the Soulfire Vessel. With Valus Tau'rig dead, you can claim the Cull's Shadow Exotic.

You can now end the Exotic Mission by heading to the center of the room before you fought Valus and interacting with the circle.

There are Catalysts to uncover in Oblation: Bloodline now that you have Cull's Shadow, and we'll cover those in a separate guide that we'll tag here soon!

Looking for more things to do in Destiny 2 's final live-service update? Why not try to unlock the Immortal Title, the final title that will ever be released! Or, you can try your luck at Sparrow Racing League to champion its loot pool and Triumphs!

Destiny 2

Follow
Followed
FPS
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 84 /100 Critics Rec: 88%
Released
August 28, 2017
ESRB
T For TEEN for Blood, Language, and Violence
Developer(s)
Bungie
Publisher(s)
Bungie

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki

Genre(s)
FPS
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
Bloodline: Oblation is a secret Exotic Mission in Destiny 2 that takes you through a Hive fortress that's been infiltrated by Dredgeb Bael and the remaining forces of his Barant Imperium.

The hunt for Dredgen Call's long-lost gun begins, and with winding caverns and confusing mechanics, you might need a guide to help you reach the finish line to get Cull's Shadow (an Exotic Kinetic Fusion Rifle) for yourself.

Haven't got Oblation Bloodline unlocked? Click the link provided below for a guide on unlocking the mission, as this guide covers how to complete the mission itself.

How to Sabotage the Syndicate Drilling Operation in Destiny 2

After a rendezvous with Aunor, you will need to head down to the Hive structures to sabotage the Syndicate Drilling Operation. To begin, look to the left of the giant laser, and you'll find a room containing a Totality Enforcer. With him dead, you can engage the Manual Safety Override, which reveals three blue Cooling Tower Control Fuses you need to shoot .

How to Destroy the Cooling Pillars in Destiny 2

Cooling Pillar #1

You now need to return to the other platform, as this has the first Cooling Pillar that you need to destroy. The central device has four vents that open randomly, and when they're open, you can shoot them to destroy them. Once all four have been shot, use the launch pad to reach the next area.

Cooling Pillar #2 & #3

Once you've landed on the new platform, you'll want to eliminate the Totality Enforcer and immediately turn around to find another terminal you can interact with. This will open three more Cooling Tower Control Fuses on the left, right, and middle towers to destroy.

With these fuses destroyed, you can now shoot the 4 vents on the Cooling Pillar to the left and right. With them destroyed, it's time to move onto the path on the left, which has a room filled with Legion to kill. With them dead, interact with the terminal that says "WARNING: OVERLOAD", and take the launch pad to the next area.

How to Brave the Tunnels in Destiny 2

Once you cross over to the other side of the Hive fortress, you'll be attacked by heaps of Thrall and Acolytes. Once they're dead, you're going to find many tunnels, but they will all lead to dead ends. Instead, you need to navigate all the way to the top . At the very top, you can enter the tunnels through to the Soulfire Crux.

When you see Dredgen Bael's shadow, you can safely ignore it and instead look to your left to see Hive lanterns that you can jump on. Your goal is to platform all the way across to the other side and up the platforms to reach a red room, where you can drop down to a ledge on the other side to enter the room.

Follow the hallway down to find Bael's shadow again, but the room you enter will be locked. Turn around and look down the hallway, and you can find a crack in the stairs that you can drop down to find the next area. You can now continue on the path until you find the Soulfire Barrier.

How to Extinguish the Soulfire Barrier in Destiny 2

Once you kill the Hive enemies, there will be a Wizard called the Pyre Matron. You must kill the Pyre Matron to release the shields on the Soulfire Vessel . You can now kill the Soulfire Vessel with a melee or finisher to get the Soulfire Charge . Take the Soulfire Charge to the unlit pyre to ignite it, and repeat this in the left and right rooms.

The left room is accessed via the rock platforms instead of through a door.

How to Beat Valus Tau'rig in Destiny 2

You'll now be at the boss encounter against Valus Tau'rig, who is using Cull's Shadow. Valus has a first phase where you just need to fight him as normal. However, before you can kill him, Hive Wizards corrupt him, and thus begins his next phase.

To beat Valus Tau'rig, you need to kill ads until you see the text "He seeks a source of soulfire". Now, in either the left or right room, there will be Pyre Matrons and a Soulfire Vessel. You just need to kill them and light one of the three pyres with the Soulfire Charge to lower his shields .

Subscribe to the newsletter for more Destiny 2 guides

Get more from the game by subscribing to the newsletter for focused Destiny 2 coverage: precise mission walkthroughs, step-by-step tactics for encounters, and follow-up guides on Exotics and catalysts — everything tied to in-game unlocks like Cull's Shadow.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

For the second-half of his health bar, you will need to use a Soulfire Charge on two pyres, so follow the Hive Wizards into the ritual room and kill them so you can then kill the Soulfire Vessel. With Valus Tau'rig dead, you can claim the Cull's Shadow Exotic.

You can now end the Exotic Mission by heading to the center of the room before you fought Valus and interacting with the circle.

There are Catalysts to uncover in Oblation: Bloodline now that you have Cull's Shadow, and we'll cover those in a separate guide that we'll tag here soon!

Looking for more things to do in Destiny 2 's final live-service update? Why not try to unlock the Immortal Title, the final title that will ever be released! Or, you can try your luck at Sparrow Racing League to champion its loot pool and Triumphs!

item.title: 10 Biggest Games Officially Confirmed For Fall 2026
item.description:
The Summer of gaming is very much underway, and we have been treated to plenty of new reveals and additional trailers for some of the biggest games launching in the latter half of 2026. It can be hard to keep up with all the announcements and upcoming dates, especially when the year is as stacked as it is, but even though things may feel overwhelming, this fall is guaranteed to be one hell of a gaming season. Call of Duty , GTA , and Silent Hill will dominate Fall 2026.

The list of names sounds absurd on paper, with so many seasoned franchises receiving major sequels and plenty of new faces stepping up to the plate, yet despite how crazy the lineup is, there are only a few short months between now and the whole set being released into the wild. Regardless of whether you are an FPS fan or a racing lover, there is bound to be at least one game for you to wishlist and start getting excited for.

Fit the 9 games into the grid.

Criteria Grid

Pick a tile to place a game.

Time: 00:00

Results

0
—
More Games

Pick a game

Choose 1 of 9
×

Fit the 9 games into the grid.
Criteria Grid Pick a tile to place a game.
Time: 00:00
Results
0
—
More Games
Pick a game
Choose 1 of 9
×

Marvel's Wolverine

Trading Webs For Pure Adrenaline

Details:

  • Release Date: September 15th (PS5 exclusive)
  • Explosive combat with a more linear structure

Wolverine is Insomniac's next big Marvel project , looking to trade the playful energy of Spider-Man for the grittier, more brutal personality of the adamantium beast himself. You get to step into a completely new story within the mutant world, one that will have you clawing away at enemies in all manner of locations, from Tokyo to Canada, all in the name of survival.

The biggest difference between this game and many other Marvel game projects is its more linear, focused scope. The gameplay takes you through many more controlled set pieces that empower the action rather than restrict it, and I, for one, am excited to experience a less broad journey that is more interested in constant pace than large, open playing fields.

Valor Mortis

First-Person Soulslike Goodness

Details:

  • Release Date: 24th September
  • Brutal melee combat

Valor Mortis comes from the developers of Ghostrunner , and rather than being another lightning-fast action game, the focus is more on atmosphere and methodical combat. Playing as a soldier during the Napoleonic era, you get to witness the atrocities of war first-hand, equipped with a sword, a gun, and a range of special abilities that bring a supernatural edge to an already unnatural world.

Described as a first-person soulslike, the combat certainly looks and feels more rhythmic and calculated than other RPGs, with the demo giving you a glimpse of the game's style and rhythm long before launch. I am a big fan of the dev team, and from what I have played so far, it is for sure a day-one purchase that I will likely struggle to put down.

Control Resonant

Bending Every Inch Of Reality

Details:

  • ​​​​Release Date: September 24th
  • Mind-blowing set-pieces and locations

Remedy has come out with quite a few bangers over the past few years, and CONTROL Resonant is looking to be yet another name to add to that list. Moving away from the story of the first game, we get to experience a different perspective in the same universe, playing as Dylan Faden in the world outside the Oldest House that appears even more distorted than the one within.

The combat has also changed a bunch, taking a more melee-oriented route that brings with it heavier blows and more chances to demolish enemies up close and personal. I think the biggest standout has to be the setting, as while the original had plenty of incredible shots and moments, the locations here look beyond extravagant, and I am dying to explore them in all their glory.

Onimusha: Way of the Sword

Can The Capcom Streak Last Forever?

Details:

  • Release Date: September 25th
  • Challenging yet satisfying swordfights

It has been quite some time since we saw an Onimusha game, but the wait may have been more than worth it. Modernized visuals, gameplay, and story all combine to deliver an ARPG experience that is much closer to a hack-and-slash than a soulslike, thanks to the faster combat and emphasis on combos and ability usage.

The power comes from an Oni gauntlet that you get to wield and use against a wide range of enemies, from skilled swordsmen to giant, lumbering ogres, each with a new challenge that requires patience and timing to overcome. The gameplay alone has me hooked, but I think world-building is going to be a big deal in setting it apart from the competition, taking notes from all parts of Japanese folklore to create a distinctive feel that should be unlike any other in the genre.

SILENT HILL: Townfall

The Mist Continues To Spread

Details:

  • Release Date: 24th September
  • A new location with a whole new set of horrors

SILENT HILL: Townfall is yet another brand-new game in the iconic horror franchise that is stepping into a very different setting, whilst still keeping the eerie mist in full view. An abandoned island town full of mysteries and unanswered questions, your main tools to survive will be your senses and a handy CRTV that can pick up on minute details that may be the difference between life and death.

I am mainly interested in the first-person perspective, as much like RE7 , it feels like a reinvention of an existing formula that may well prove to be just as good, if not better, than the original. But, even with these changes and a new environment to explore, you will still have to contend with the same old nail-biting tension that will inevitably be broken by an enemy charging your way.

Phantom Blade Zero

Fighting Till Your Final Days

Details:

  • Release Date: October 29th
  • Heavy focus on different styles of martial arts

Phantom Blade Zero is probably at the top of many people's wishlists for 2026, and it has earned that spot for good reason. Yes, it is another ARPG from Eastern Asia, but the presentation is much darker and more brutal, aided primarily by the unique narrative that follows a warrior in his quest to fend off death.

When it comes to the gameplay, they could have played it safe and gone for a standard soulslike or basic RPG style, but instead, the developers opted for a faithful homage to Wuxia fighting styles that are bolstered by an arsenal of unique weapons, each with their own style that can be used to adapt to each situation. Hyped doesn't even begin to describe my feelings, and despite being delayed slightly, I would much prefer a few extra weeks of polish so we can really experience the height of what the game has to offer.

Call Of Duty: Modern Warfare 4

The King Of FPSs For A Reason

Details:

  • Release Date: 23rd October
  • Set during a war between North Korea and the rest of the world

Number jokes and repetition aside, Modern Warfare actually looks like a pretty interesting COD game that might actually usher in a new wave of excitement for the franchise. The conflict is global, taking place in Korea, Paris, Mumbai, and more, but the classic FPS gameplay remains mostly intact, albeit in a darker, more intense form than previous entries.

Alongside a whole new campaign and a classic multiplayer gameplay style, we are also going to get to play DMZ once again, a separate extraction-style mode that requires far more strategy and game sense for those looking to come out on top. I have been a bit distant from the more recent CODs , but MW4 looks pretty damn interesting and may be just what I and many others needed to get back on the iconic FPS bandwagon.

STAR WARS: Galactic Racer

Now This Is Pod Racing

Details:

  • Release Date: 6th October
  • Cinematic races, both online and in the story

If Forza Horizon 6 wasn't your cup of tea, then maybe it's time to enter a time long, long ago with STAR WARS: Galactic Racer . In a post-Empire galaxy, a league of racing has been created, full of hungry drivers and steep competition that offers glory to the victors and certain death to the losers.

Not only do we get to charge across various planets against other players, but there is also a dedicated story that adds a nice backstory to the experience that many other racing games struggle to nail. If you are someone who has just picked up a new wheel or are just a big STAR WARS fan , then this new entry feels like the ideal place to capture some of the less explored areas of the franchise and to prove just how good a driver you really are.

Ace Combat 8: Wings Of Theve

Taking To The Skies Once More

Details:

  • Release Date: 2nd October
  • Breathtaking aerial gameplay

​​​​​​​

Ace Combat 8: Wings Of Theve takes the action into the clouds, presenting you with an array of breathtaking sky-bound fights that look like something straight out of a movie. The key point comes from the relationship between the air and ground, as while flying around is all well and good, you will still need to form important connections and make tough choices when standing on your own two feet.

If the campaign wasn't enough, there are also chances to play online with other pilots, adding an extra level of excitement to those crazy dogfights. I have followed the series for a while and am delighted to see a brand-new installment with a greater focus on spectacle and to have more chances to fly alongside my friends online.

Grand Theft Auto 6

The Wait Is Almost Over

Details:

  • ​​​​​​​Release Date: 19th November (PS5/Xbox Series)
  • Dual protagonists in a fresh new world

Assuming it doesn't get a last-minute delay again, you might finally get to play GTA6 in 2026. A Bonnie and Clyde-style story that brings with it the franchise's first female protagonist, Lucia, and her partner in crime, Jason, we are going to explore a vibrant, Florida-inspired city that should look and feel as close to reality as humanly possible.

The development time and costs for this game are absurd, and with a name as big as Rockstar at the helm, it is very clear we are in for a generational experience that may well eclipse the entire gaming industry for years to come. I, among others, will be rushing through the doors the first chance I can, and if you are a gamer, even in the slightest bit, you owe it to yourself to get excited as well.

​​​​​​​
item.content:encoded:
The Summer of gaming is very much underway, and we have been treated to plenty of new reveals and additional trailers for some of the biggest games launching in the latter half of 2026. It can be hard to keep up with all the announcements and upcoming dates, especially when the year is as stacked as it is, but even though things may feel overwhelming, this fall is guaranteed to be one hell of a gaming season. Call of Duty , GTA , and Silent Hill will dominate Fall 2026.

The list of names sounds absurd on paper, with so many seasoned franchises receiving major sequels and plenty of new faces stepping up to the plate, yet despite how crazy the lineup is, there are only a few short months between now and the whole set being released into the wild. Regardless of whether you are an FPS fan or a racing lover, there is bound to be at least one game for you to wishlist and start getting excited for.

Fit the 9 games into the grid.

Criteria Grid

Pick a tile to place a game.

Time: 00:00

Results

0
—
More Games

Pick a game

Choose 1 of 9
×

Fit the 9 games into the grid.
Criteria Grid Pick a tile to place a game.
Time: 00:00
Results
0
—
More Games
Pick a game
Choose 1 of 9
×

Marvel's Wolverine

Trading Webs For Pure Adrenaline

Details:

  • Release Date: September 15th (PS5 exclusive)
  • Explosive combat with a more linear structure

Wolverine is Insomniac's next big Marvel project , looking to trade the playful energy of Spider-Man for the grittier, more brutal personality of the adamantium beast himself. You get to step into a completely new story within the mutant world, one that will have you clawing away at enemies in all manner of locations, from Tokyo to Canada, all in the name of survival.

The biggest difference between this game and many other Marvel game projects is its more linear, focused scope. The gameplay takes you through many more controlled set pieces that empower the action rather than restrict it, and I, for one, am excited to experience a less broad journey that is more interested in constant pace than large, open playing fields.

Valor Mortis

First-Person Soulslike Goodness

Details:

  • Release Date: 24th September
  • Brutal melee combat

Valor Mortis comes from the developers of Ghostrunner , and rather than being another lightning-fast action game, the focus is more on atmosphere and methodical combat. Playing as a soldier during the Napoleonic era, you get to witness the atrocities of war first-hand, equipped with a sword, a gun, and a range of special abilities that bring a supernatural edge to an already unnatural world.

Described as a first-person soulslike, the combat certainly looks and feels more rhythmic and calculated than other RPGs, with the demo giving you a glimpse of the game's style and rhythm long before launch. I am a big fan of the dev team, and from what I have played so far, it is for sure a day-one purchase that I will likely struggle to put down.

Control Resonant

Bending Every Inch Of Reality

Details:

  • ​​​​Release Date: September 24th
  • Mind-blowing set-pieces and locations

Remedy has come out with quite a few bangers over the past few years, and CONTROL Resonant is looking to be yet another name to add to that list. Moving away from the story of the first game, we get to experience a different perspective in the same universe, playing as Dylan Faden in the world outside the Oldest House that appears even more distorted than the one within.

The combat has also changed a bunch, taking a more melee-oriented route that brings with it heavier blows and more chances to demolish enemies up close and personal. I think the biggest standout has to be the setting, as while the original had plenty of incredible shots and moments, the locations here look beyond extravagant, and I am dying to explore them in all their glory.

Onimusha: Way of the Sword

Can The Capcom Streak Last Forever?

Details:

  • Release Date: September 25th
  • Challenging yet satisfying swordfights

It has been quite some time since we saw an Onimusha game, but the wait may have been more than worth it. Modernized visuals, gameplay, and story all combine to deliver an ARPG experience that is much closer to a hack-and-slash than a soulslike, thanks to the faster combat and emphasis on combos and ability usage.

The power comes from an Oni gauntlet that you get to wield and use against a wide range of enemies, from skilled swordsmen to giant, lumbering ogres, each with a new challenge that requires patience and timing to overcome. The gameplay alone has me hooked, but I think world-building is going to be a big deal in setting it apart from the competition, taking notes from all parts of Japanese folklore to create a distinctive feel that should be unlike any other in the genre.

SILENT HILL: Townfall

The Mist Continues To Spread

Details:

  • Release Date: 24th September
  • A new location with a whole new set of horrors

SILENT HILL: Townfall is yet another brand-new game in the iconic horror franchise that is stepping into a very different setting, whilst still keeping the eerie mist in full view. An abandoned island town full of mysteries and unanswered questions, your main tools to survive will be your senses and a handy CRTV that can pick up on minute details that may be the difference between life and death.

I am mainly interested in the first-person perspective, as much like RE7 , it feels like a reinvention of an existing formula that may well prove to be just as good, if not better, than the original. But, even with these changes and a new environment to explore, you will still have to contend with the same old nail-biting tension that will inevitably be broken by an enemy charging your way.

Phantom Blade Zero

Fighting Till Your Final Days

Details:

  • Release Date: October 29th
  • Heavy focus on different styles of martial arts

Phantom Blade Zero is probably at the top of many people's wishlists for 2026, and it has earned that spot for good reason. Yes, it is another ARPG from Eastern Asia, but the presentation is much darker and more brutal, aided primarily by the unique narrative that follows a warrior in his quest to fend off death.

When it comes to the gameplay, they could have played it safe and gone for a standard soulslike or basic RPG style, but instead, the developers opted for a faithful homage to Wuxia fighting styles that are bolstered by an arsenal of unique weapons, each with their own style that can be used to adapt to each situation. Hyped doesn't even begin to describe my feelings, and despite being delayed slightly, I would much prefer a few extra weeks of polish so we can really experience the height of what the game has to offer.

Call Of Duty: Modern Warfare 4

The King Of FPSs For A Reason

Details:

  • Release Date: 23rd October
  • Set during a war between North Korea and the rest of the world

Number jokes and repetition aside, Modern Warfare actually looks like a pretty interesting COD game that might actually usher in a new wave of excitement for the franchise. The conflict is global, taking place in Korea, Paris, Mumbai, and more, but the classic FPS gameplay remains mostly intact, albeit in a darker, more intense form than previous entries.

Alongside a whole new campaign and a classic multiplayer gameplay style, we are also going to get to play DMZ once again, a separate extraction-style mode that requires far more strategy and game sense for those looking to come out on top. I have been a bit distant from the more recent CODs , but MW4 looks pretty damn interesting and may be just what I and many others needed to get back on the iconic FPS bandwagon.

STAR WARS: Galactic Racer

Now This Is Pod Racing

Details:

  • Release Date: 6th October
  • Cinematic races, both online and in the story

If Forza Horizon 6 wasn't your cup of tea, then maybe it's time to enter a time long, long ago with STAR WARS: Galactic Racer . In a post-Empire galaxy, a league of racing has been created, full of hungry drivers and steep competition that offers glory to the victors and certain death to the losers.

Not only do we get to charge across various planets against other players, but there is also a dedicated story that adds a nice backstory to the experience that many other racing games struggle to nail. If you are someone who has just picked up a new wheel or are just a big STAR WARS fan , then this new entry feels like the ideal place to capture some of the less explored areas of the franchise and to prove just how good a driver you really are.

Ace Combat 8: Wings Of Theve

Taking To The Skies Once More

Details:

  • Release Date: 2nd October
  • Breathtaking aerial gameplay

​​​​​​​

Ace Combat 8: Wings Of Theve takes the action into the clouds, presenting you with an array of breathtaking sky-bound fights that look like something straight out of a movie. The key point comes from the relationship between the air and ground, as while flying around is all well and good, you will still need to form important connections and make tough choices when standing on your own two feet.

If the campaign wasn't enough, there are also chances to play online with other pilots, adding an extra level of excitement to those crazy dogfights. I have followed the series for a while and am delighted to see a brand-new installment with a greater focus on spectacle and to have more chances to fly alongside my friends online.

Grand Theft Auto 6

The Wait Is Almost Over

Details:

  • ​​​​​​​Release Date: 19th November (PS5/Xbox Series)
  • Dual protagonists in a fresh new world

Assuming it doesn't get a last-minute delay again, you might finally get to play GTA6 in 2026. A Bonnie and Clyde-style story that brings with it the franchise's first female protagonist, Lucia, and her partner in crime, Jason, we are going to explore a vibrant, Florida-inspired city that should look and feel as close to reality as humanly possible.

The development time and costs for this game are absurd, and with a name as big as Rockstar at the helm, it is very clear we are in for a generational experience that may well eclipse the entire gaming industry for years to come. I, among others, will be rushing through the doors the first chance I can, and if you are a gamer, even in the slightest bit, you owe it to yourself to get excited as well.

​​​​​​​

item.title: Roblox: Life Incremental Codes (June 2026)
item.description:
Life Incremental is a text-based fantasy RPG. Despite its simple design, it's incredibly engaging, boasting numerous features and allowing players to create unique characters. To progress even faster, you can use Life Incremental codes.

Developers release Roblox codes to reward players for their activity. They can contain various rewards, including temporary boosters. You'll want to hurry to redeem these codes, as they have a limited validity period.

Updated June 10, 2026, by Artur Novichenko: Several new codes have recently been added, giving you extra boosts to help with progression. The latest additions provide free bonuses that can make advancing through the game more efficient and rewarding. Older codes are still available as well, offering additional rewards and useful effects. With multiple active codes to redeem, there are plenty of bonuses worth picking up right now. Be sure to check back later for new codes and future updates to the list.

All Life Incremental Codes

Working Life Incremental Codes

  • Update18 - Redeem this code to get free boosts. (NEW)
  • NewArea - Redeem this code to get free boosts. (NEW)
  • Almost5Mil​​​​​ - Redeem this code to get free boosts. (NEW)
  • Ingredients - Redeem this code to get free​​​​​​​ boosts. (NEW)
  • Doublexp - Redeem this code to get free rewards
  • Money - Redeem this code to get free rewards

Expired Life Incremental Codes

  • ThousandPlayers
  • update9
  • life9
  • coolbugfixes
  • Update8
  • WowFastUpdate
  • ThanksFor2Mil
  • 2Million
  • Update7
  • spooky
  • bugfix

In Life Incremental , players begin their adventure at birth. After that, you'll need to learn and train to increase your stats. As befits the genre, most of your actions will generate Cash, which you can use to unlock new locations, learn skills, and progress through the story. However, initially, players generate Сash extremely slowly. To double this, you'll want to use Life Incremental codes.

Each promo code in Life Incremental grants players valuable rewards. These include currencies and boosters. The latter are especially useful for beginners, as they can double your income, albeit for a short time. However, if you delay using them, they will expire, and the rewards will no longer be available to you.

How to Redeem Life Incremental Codes

To redeem Life Incremental codes, you only need a few clicks, as with most other Roblox games. Note that some codes have hidden restrictions. You may need to reach certain locations or learn certain skills to claim rewards. However, most codes are available without any conditions.

  • Launch the Life Incremental experience.
  • Next, progress until you unlock Settings.
  • Open it and enter the code in the appropriate field.
  • Finally, click Redeem to receive rewards.

If you haven't met the requirements, you'll see a notification appear.

How to Get More Life Incremental Codes

To receive Life Incremental codes, bookmark this guide by pressing CTRL+D as soon as they're redeemed. We'll update it over time, like our other Roblox guides. Players can also follow the developer's official Discord server. There you'll find all the news about upcoming updates and giveaways:

  • Life Incremental Discord server
Roblox
Released
September 1, 2006

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
Teen / Diverse Content: Discretion Advised, In-Game Purchases (Includes Random Items), Users Interact
Genre(s)
Game Creation System
Platform(s)
PlayStation 4, Xbox One, PC, Android, iOS, Meta Quest 2, Meta Quest Pro
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
Life Incremental is a text-based fantasy RPG. Despite its simple design, it's incredibly engaging, boasting numerous features and allowing players to create unique characters. To progress even faster, you can use Life Incremental codes.

Developers release Roblox codes to reward players for their activity. They can contain various rewards, including temporary boosters. You'll want to hurry to redeem these codes, as they have a limited validity period.

Updated June 10, 2026, by Artur Novichenko: Several new codes have recently been added, giving you extra boosts to help with progression. The latest additions provide free bonuses that can make advancing through the game more efficient and rewarding. Older codes are still available as well, offering additional rewards and useful effects. With multiple active codes to redeem, there are plenty of bonuses worth picking up right now. Be sure to check back later for new codes and future updates to the list.

All Life Incremental Codes

Working Life Incremental Codes

  • Update18 - Redeem this code to get free boosts. (NEW)
  • NewArea - Redeem this code to get free boosts. (NEW)
  • Almost5Mil​​​​​ - Redeem this code to get free boosts. (NEW)
  • Ingredients - Redeem this code to get free​​​​​​​ boosts. (NEW)
  • Doublexp - Redeem this code to get free rewards
  • Money - Redeem this code to get free rewards

  • update9
  • life9
  • coolbugfixes
  • Update8
  • WowFastUpdate
  • ThanksFor2Mil
  • 2Million
  • Update7
  • spooky
  • bugfix

In Life Incremental , players begin their adventure at birth. After that, you'll need to learn and train to increase your stats. As befits the genre, most of your actions will generate Cash, which you can use to unlock new locations, learn skills, and progress through the story. However, initially, players generate Сash extremely slowly. To double this, you'll want to use Life Incremental codes.

Each promo code in Life Incremental grants players valuable rewards. These include currencies and boosters. The latter are especially useful for beginners, as they can double your income, albeit for a short time. However, if you delay using them, they will expire, and the rewards will no longer be available to you.

How to Redeem Life Incremental Codes

To redeem Life Incremental codes, you only need a few clicks, as with most other Roblox games. Note that some codes have hidden restrictions. You may need to reach certain locations or learn certain skills to claim rewards. However, most codes are available without any conditions.

  • Launch the Life Incremental experience.
  • Next, progress until you unlock Settings.
  • Open it and enter the code in the appropriate field.
  • Finally, click Redeem to receive rewards.

If you haven't met the requirements, you'll see a notification appear.

How to Get More Life Incremental Codes

To receive Life Incremental codes, bookmark this guide by pressing CTRL+D as soon as they're redeemed. We'll update it over time, like our other Roblox guides. Players can also follow the developer's official Discord server. There you'll find all the news about upcoming updates and giveaways:

  • Life Incremental Discord server

item.title: Roblox Elementalism Codes (June 2026)
item.description:
Elementalism is a super cool Roblox game where you get to dive into a big world full of action and adventure! Codes are really important in Elementalism because they hand out free rewards that make everything more fun without all the extra grinding.

Whether it's cool accessories for your avatar or boosts to help you level up faster, codes have the power to give you all this and more. Our list of updated codes will help you jump right into the best parts of the game!

Updated June 10, 2026, by Artur Novichenko: A couple of new codes have recently been added, bringing some useful rewards to claim. The latest additions provide Aureus and a Rare Trait Reroll, giving you both currency and character customization options. Several older codes are still active as well, offering additional Aureus, Mastery, Spirituality, and cosmetic items. With a variety of rewards available, now is a good time to redeem everything on the list. Be sure to check back later for new codes and future updates.

All Elementalism Codes

Working Elementalism Codes

Code

Reward(s)

UPD15

1,000 Aureus (NEW)

Conjurer!

Rare Trait Reroll (NEW)

Warrior!

Rare Trait Reroll

WBrazil

Brazilian Fedora

WBrazil2

Brazilian Cap

UPD14

750 Aureus

FreeMastery

5 Mastery

FreeSpirituality

5 Spirituality

MoneyBoost

1,000 Aureus

1410Spirituality

5 Spirituality

Expired Elementalism Codes

Sometimes, codes can stop working. If that's the case, we will move them here, so you don't try them.

The active codes are awesome for giving you quick wins. Promo codes like these help you score extra coins to buy gear, mastery points to get stronger faster, and spirituality boosts for better skills. Accessories make your character look unique, too. It's a smart way to keep the excitement going strong without waiting hours to earn everything on your own.

How to Redeem Elementalism Codes

  • Join the official Roblox community of the game.
  • Open Elementalism in Roblox to jump into the game.
  • Tap the menu button up at the top-left corner of your screen.
  • Switch over to the blue profile tab.
  • Find the redeem codes spot and type each one in carefully, exactly as shown.
  • Hit redeem and watch your free rewards pop up instantly!

How to Get More Elementalism Codes

Head straight to the official Elementalism Discord server for the freshest updates straight from the devs. You can also join their Roblox group to stay in the loop on everything happening in this Roblox game.

We check for new codes all the time and refresh this list whenever something drops, so popping back here every few days is the smartest move for getting new ones. With this guide, you always catch the good stuff before it goes away and keep your adventures rolling smoothly!

Roblox
Released
September 1, 2006

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
Teen / Diverse Content: Discretion Advised, In-Game Purchases (Includes Random Items), Users Interact
Genre(s)
Game Creation System
Platform(s)
PlayStation 4, Xbox One, PC, Android, iOS, Meta Quest 2, Meta Quest Pro
Powered by

Elementalism is a super cool Roblox game where you get to dive into a big world full of action and adventure! Codes are really important in Elementalism because they hand out free rewards that make everything more fun without all the extra grinding.

Whether it's cool accessories for your avatar or boosts to help you level up faster, codes have the power to give you all this and more. Our list of updated codes will help you jump right into the best parts of the game!

Updated June 10, 2026, by Artur Novichenko: A couple of new codes have recently been added, bringing some useful rewards to claim. The latest additions provide Aureus and a Rare Trait Reroll, giving you both currency and character customization options. Several older codes are still active as well, offering additional Aureus, Mastery, Spirituality, and cosmetic items. With a variety of rewards available, now is a good time to redeem everything on the list. Be sure to check back later for new codes and future updates.

All Elementalism Codes

Promo codes like these help you score extra coins to buy gear, mastery points to get stronger faster, and spirituality boosts for better skills. Accessories make your character look unique, too. How to Redeem Elementalism Codes

  • Switch over to the blue profile tab.
